# Mona Mayhem Copilot Instructions

## Theme
Mona Mayhem is a retro-neon battle arena game with a cyberpunk arcade aesthetic.

## Visual Style
- Use a dark navy or near-black background.
- Use neon blue, electric purple, and magenta as accent colors.
- Use glowing borders, bright call-to-action buttons, and arcade-inspired headings.
- Keep panels rounded with a subtle glassmorphism effect.

## Layout Rules
- Prefer CSS grid for major page layouts.
- Keep spacing generous and sections visually separated with cards or arena panels.
- Layouts should be responsive on desktop and mobile.

## UI Language
Use battle-themed labels when appropriate, such as:
- Arena
- Power-Up
- Match History
- Combo
- Victory
- Defeat
- Game Over
- Replay
- Return to Arena

## Component Rules
- Buttons should be high-contrast and visually prominent.
- Cards should have clear titles and compact descriptions.
- Important status text should stand out clearly.
- Prefer reusable React functional components.

## Code Style
- Use React functional components.
- Keep components readable and split logic into small sections when useful.
- Prefer simple state management with hooks.
- Write clean and beginner-friendly code.

## Screen Behavior
When creating new screens, especially a Game Over or Battle Summary screen:
- Show a strong headline
- Show the match result clearly
- Include one or two action buttons
- Keep the UI polished, game-like, and visually consistent with the retro-neon theme# Mona Mayhem Copilot Instructions

## Theme
Mona Mayhem is a retro-neon battle arena game with a cyberpunk arcade aesthetic.

## Visual Style
- Use a dark navy or near-black background.
- Use neon blue, electric purple, and magenta as accent colors.
- Use glowing borders, bright call-to-action buttons, and arcade-inspired headings.
- Keep panels rounded with a subtle glassmorphism effect.

## Layout Rules
- Prefer CSS grid for major page layouts.
- Keep spacing generous and sections visually separated with cards or arena panels.
- Layouts should be responsive on desktop and mobile.

## UI Language
Use battle-themed labels when appropriate, such as:
- Arena
- Power-Up
- Match History
- Combo
- Victory
- Defeat
- Game Over
- Replay
- Return to Arena

## Component Rules
- Buttons should be high-contrast and visually prominent.
- Cards should have clear titles and compact descriptions.
- Important status text should stand out clearly.
- Prefer reusable React functional components.

## Code Style
- Use React functional components.
- Keep components readable and split logic into small sections when useful.
- Prefer simple state management with hooks.
- Write clean and beginner-friendly code.

## Screen Behavior
When creating new screens, especially a Game Over or Battle Summary screen:
- Show a strong headline
- Show the match result clearly
- Include one or two action buttons
- Keep the UI polished, game-like, and visually consistent with the retro-neon theme
